primary_marker.rationale: Creatine kinase rises with skeletal muscle membrane disruption but requires contextual interpretation because exercise and non-neuromuscular factors can cause transient elevation.

supporting_markers.rationale: Mild concurrent AST elevation supports a skeletal-muscle source of enzyme release when the hepatic picture is otherwise unconvincing.
Troponin helps distinguish isolated skeletal-muscle CK release from a cardiac-injury pattern that should not be grouped into the same frame.

hypotheses.physiological_claim: High CK may reflect recent exertional or mechanical skeletal muscle injury, particularly when the elevation is transient and other muscle-associated enzymes are also mildly raised.
caveats: Recent vigorous exercise, trauma, injections, or seizures should be reviewed before inferring intrinsic muscle disease.

missing_data.policy: Repeat CK after a period without strenuous exercise and review recent physical stressors before escalating interpretation.

contradiction_markers.contradiction_rationale: Troponin elevation shifts concern away from isolated skeletal muscle release and toward cardiac injury assessment.

hypotheses.physiological_claim: High CK may represent a transient non-neuromuscular CK elevation rather than a primary myopathic process when symptoms and repeat abnormalities are absent.
caveats: A single isolated CK elevation has limited specificity for chronic muscle disease.

missing_data.policy: Confirm persistence on repeat testing and review medications, endocrine status, alcohol use, and recent exertion before assigning a chronic disease frame.

contradiction_markers.contradiction_rationale: Inflammatory marker elevation can increase concern for a more sustained inflammatory or systemic process rather than a purely transient exertional rise.

confirmatory_tests.rationale: Repeating CK after a short period of exercise avoidance helps distinguish transient exertional release from persistent non-exertional CK elevation.

override_rules.description: Escalate when high CK coincides with high AST, as this more strongly supports active tissue injury than an isolated CK rise.

sources.paper_title: Approach to asymptomatic creatine kinase elevation

evidence.physiological_claim: CK elevation is common after exercise and other non-neuromuscular stressors, so persistence and context matter before inferring primary muscle disease.
evidence.threshold_notes: Condition-specific escalation thresholds are not robustly uniform across populations; interpretation should remain lab-range anchored and contextual.

narrative.mechanism: CK is released from damaged skeletal muscle fibers and can rise transiently after exertion, trauma, or other mechanical muscle stress.
narrative.biological_pathway: Skeletal muscle membrane injury and intracellular enzyme release into the circulation.
narrative.interpretation: An isolated high CK is more consistent with recent muscle stress than with a chronic myopathic process, particularly when the elevation is transient and context is benign.
narrative.implications: Repeat testing after rest and review of exertional and medication history usually help determine whether the abnormality is transient or persistent.
narrative.supporting_marker_roles: AST corroborates a muscle-source enzyme leak, while troponin functions as the key differential marker separating exertional skeletal-muscle release from cardiac injury.
